data.title: Hut 8's Bitcoin Bonanza: $331M Net Income, 10,171 BTC Reserve
data.description:
Hut 8, a leading Bitcoin mining firm, reported a net income of $331 million in 2024, as per its latest financial report. The company's significant earnings can be attributed to the substantial increase in Bitcoin's price throughout the year.

By the end of 2024, hut 8 had amassed a reserve of 10,171 Bitcoin, valued at approximately $905 million at the time of writing. The majority of this reserve has been pledged as collateral to facilitate the purchase of additional ASIC mining machines, indicating the company's commitment to expanding its mining capabilities.

Hut 8 experienced a notable reduction in energy costs during the fourth quarter, with costs per megawatt-hour dropping by 30% compared to the previous year, reaching $31.63. Despite managing around 1,020 MW at the end of December, the company has a pipeline of more than 12,300 MW, demonstrating its potential for future growth.

The miner strengthened its ties with Bitmain, one of the world's largest Bitcoin mining firms. Hut 8 secured a colocation deal from Bitmain, expected to generate $125 million in annual revenue, and is also collaborating with the firm to develop a next-generation ASIC miner. This strategic partnership is likely to enhance Hut 8's competitive position in the Bitcoin mining industry.

Hut 8 is also increasing its investments in AI infrastructure. Its subsidiary, Highrise AI, signed a five-year customer agreement for GPU-as-a-Service, and the company closed a $150 million strategic investment from Coatue to support AI development. These moves suggest that Hut 8 is diversifying its business model to capitalize on emerging opportunities in the AI sector.

Despite the positive financial performance and strategic developments, Hut 8's stock experienced a decline of 7.25% on the day, bringing its valuation to $1.5 billion. This temporary setback may be attributed to broader market conditions or investor sentiment, but it does not detract from the company's overall progress and potential.

data.title: SEC Drops Lawsuit Against Kraken, Clearing Path for U.S. Crypto Innovation
data.description:
The Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) has agreed to drop its lawsuit against cryptocurrency exchange Kraken, marking a significant shift in the regulatory landscape for the digital asset industry in the United States. The decision, which does not involve any admissions of wrongdoing, penalties, or alterations to Kraken's business operations, comes as a relief to the company and the broader cryptocurrency community.

Kraken, which has maintained its innocence throughout the legal battle, welcomed the news as a confirmation of its commitment to integrity and compliance. The company has long argued that the SEC's lawsuit was politically motivated and did not serve to protect investors. Instead, it added confusion to an emerging industry seeking clear regulations.

The dismissal of the lawsuit removes a cloud of uncertainty that had been hindering innovation and investment in the cryptocurrency sector. Kraken expressed appreciation for the new leadership at the White House and the Commission, attributing the change in approach to their bold and thoughtful leadership. The company anticipates that this development will usher in a new era of cryptocurrency innovation in the U.S.

Kraken sees cryptocurrency as a pathway to financial empowerment, with millions of Americans relying on the platform to access digital assets and manage their financial journeys. The company believes that regulatory overreach not only harms businesses but also limits opportunities for everyday people seeking alternative financial tools.

While the dismissal of the lawsuit is a significant victory, it also serves as a call to action for more clear and forward-thinking regulatory policies. Kraken remains committed to working with policymakers and regulators to establish guidelines that protect consumers while fostering technological progress. The company is optimistic about the efforts of Congress and Commissioner Peirce's Crypto task Force to put in place real, forward-thinking legislation and regulation, preventing a return to regulation by enforcement.

As this chapter closes, Kraken reaffirms its commitment to promoting a future where innovation and responsible regulation coexist. The company will continue to engage with industry stakeholders and regulatory bodies to promote clarity, fairness, and progress in digital finance. The journey is far from over, and the path ahead is one of continued innovation, strategic collaboration, and unwavering dedication to building a more inclusive financial future.

data.title: Nvidia's Blackwell AI Supercomputers: A Driver for Long-Term Growth
data.description:
Nvidia (NASDAQ: NVDA) has been on a tear in recent years, driven by the insatiable demand for its AI-ready products. The company's latest earnings report, released on March 3, 2025, highlighted the continued demand for its Blackwell AI supercomputers, which are designed to power advanced AI models and deliver AI software, a process often called inference. This article explores the impact of the continued demand for Nvidia's Blackwell AI supercomputers on the company's long-term growth prospects.



Nvidia's CEO, Jensen Huang, described the demand for Blackwell as "amazing," noting that the company had successfully ramped up the massive-scale production of these AI supercomputers, achieving billions of dollars in sales in its first quarter. The company's data center business, which includes sales of the Blackwell platform, reported record revenue of $35.6 billion in the fourth quarter, up 93% on an annual basis. This growth is a testament to the strong demand for Nvidia's AI-ready products and the company's ability to capitalize on the growing AI market.



The growing AI market presents significant opportunities for nvidia, with AI data center spending projected to reach $2 trillion in the next five years alone. As AI models become more sophisticated, the demand for high-performance computing solutions like Nvidia's Blackwell AI supercomputers is likely to increase. New ways of running AI models, such as long-thinking, reasoning AI, can require up to 100 times more compute per task compared to one-shot inferences. This means that as AI models become more complex, the demand for Nvidia's high-performance computing solutions will likely increase, driving the company's long-term growth.

However, there are potential risks and challenges associated with the production and supply chain of Nvidia's Blackwell platform that could impact the company's financial performance. These include supply chain issues, production delays, reliance on key customers, competition, and geopolitical risks. To mitigate these risks, Nvidia should focus on improving its supply chain management, diversifying its customer base, investing in research and development to stay ahead of the competition, and maintaining strong relationships with key customers and partners. Additionally, the company should closely monitor geopolitical developments and adapt its strategies accordingly to minimize potential disruptions.



In conclusion, the continued demand for Nvidia's Blackwell AI supercomputers, combined with the growing AI market, increasing compute requirements, diversified revenue streams, strong partnerships, and investment in research and development, positions Nvidia well for long-term growth. While there are potential risks and challenges associated with the production and supply chain of the Blackwell platform, the company's strong position in the AI market and its ability to adapt to changing market dynamics should help it overcome these obstacles and maintain its competitive edge in the long term. As Cowen analyst Joshua Buchalter noted, the Prisoner's Dilemma investment environment faced by major AI companies ensures that there is sustained demand for Nvidia's AI-ready products, further solidifying the company's strong competitive position and market share in the AI industry.

data.title: Fox Soars 1.36% on BioLife's Revenue Boost
Midland Soars 1.14% on Earnings Boost
SMCI Stock Analysis and Why I'm Still Buying
U.S. May Impose Less Than 25% Tariff on Mexico and Canada, But Trump Has Final Say
data.description:
President Donald Trump is set to impose tariffs on Mexico and Canada this Tuesday, but the exact levels are still under consideration, according to Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick.

Lutnick remarked on Fox's Sunday Morning Futures show that while Mexico and Canada have made significant efforts to address Trump's concerns regarding border crossings, they have not sufficiently tackled his worries about "fentanyl deaths in America." He added, "Trump is thinking about how exactly he wants to play it with Mexico and Canada, and that is a fluid situation."

On Sunday, Trump amended two executive orders to exclude low-value shipments from the upcoming tariffs on imports from Canada and Mexico. This decision could indicate the White House's commitment to moving forward with the tariffs, which had previously been paused for 30 days to allow for negotiations.

In addition to the tariffs on Mexico and Canada, Trump plans to impose an additional 10% tariff on China, which is already confirmed, according to Lutnick. This new tariff will be on top of the 10% tariffs that were implemented earlier this month.

Although Trump previously suggested imposing 25% tariffs on Mexico and Canada, Lutnick indicated that the final figure could be lower. His comments suggest that Mexico and Canada may make a last-ditch diplomatic effort to avert these measures, which Trump had delayed once before last month.

"He's going to think about it. He's going to put them into place on Tuesday. The Canadians and the Mexicans have been talking to him," Lutnick said.

Trump has also hinted at extending his global trade war to Canada's lumber industry after directing the Commerce Department to investigate dumping into the U.S. market.

Meanwhile, China appears to have limited options ahead of Tuesday's deadline. The country has vowed to implement countermeasures to protect its interests, having tightened controls on precursor chemicals and fentanyl-like substances.

Lutnick noted, "If they think they're going to retaliate, remember they have so much more that they sell to us than we sell to them."

data.title: Crypto.com's CRO Token: Community Rejects Burned Token Revival Plan
data.description:
Crypto.com's CRO token is facing significant backlash following a proposal to restore 70 billion burned tokens, with 87% of early voters rejecting the plan. The proposal, which aims to create a cronos Strategic Reserve, has sparked controversy among the community, with many expressing concerns about the centralization of the network.

The proposal, submitted by Cronos developers, suggests reinstating the burned tokens to create a strategic reserve. Crypto.com's CEO, Kris Marszalek, has stated that the reserve will be used to support the growth of the Cronos ecosystem and position it as the leading blockchain for AI-driven applications. If approved, the total supply of CRO would return to 100 billion, with the tokens held in an escrow wallet. The reserve would be subject to strict control measures, including a new five-year lockup period and a linear vesting mechanism that distributes monthly tokens via the Cosmos SDK on Cronos POS Chain.

The voting process is currently underway, with implementation expected to follow shortly after the conclusion of the vote on March 17, 2025. Despite the strategic goals of the proposal, it has sparked controversy among the community. Mintscan data shows that more than 500 million CRO tokens have been used in the vote, with 490 million—87%—opposing the move. Only 4%, or around 25.5 million CROs, have supported it.

The backlash has been evident across social media, with CRO advocates expressing their concerns about the centralization of the network. Wyll Bilderberg, a CRO advocate, stated that "a burn is a burn, burnt tokens shouldn't be brought back to life. I'm almost never against anything happening on Cronos, but today, I'm against it, big time! If this passes, it's just a confirmation that Cronos is heavily centralized, and so can't be trusted."

Despite the controversy, the proposal has unexpectedly impacted CRO's market performance. According to CryptoSlate data, the token surged 15% during the reporting period, reaching $0.08434 as of press time.

data.title: "1,000 XRP: The New Crypto Milestone?"
data.description:
Cryptocurrency enthusiast and CEO of Alpha Lions Academy, edo Farina, has sparked a lively debate within the crypto community by asserting that holding a minimum of 1,000 XRP tokens could be a pivotal financial decision for investors. In a recent post on X, Farina suggested that not owning at least 1,000 XRP is akin to "the definition of insanity," underscoring his conviction in the asset's future value.

Farina's rationale is rooted in the belief that XRP's utility in facilitating cross-border payments and its integration into financial systems position it for substantial appreciation. He has also highlighted the current market dynamics, noting that acquiring 1,000 XRP tokens requires a relatively modest investment. As demand for XRP increases and its adoption widens, the value of these tokens could rise significantly, making early investment advantageous.

Farina's statement has elicited varied responses from the cryptocurrency community. Some investors resonate with his optimism, adopting strategies of accumulating XRP with the expectation of long-term appreciation. For instance, a user identified as Sé real commented on Farina's post, stating, "My bags are carrying 4,546. 5,454 more to go. Hoping for some dips but anything under $5 is still cheap in the long run."

Other community members have expressed caution, acknowledging past experiences where anticipated gains did not materialize. This sentiment highlights the inherent volatility and unpredictability of the cryptocurrency market. Despite differing viewpoints, the discourse underscores a shared interest in understanding the potential risks and rewards associated with XRP investment.

An analysis of XRP ownership distribution offers additional context to Farina's assertion. According to Edo Farina in a previous tweet, holding 2,505 XRP places an investor among the top 10% of XRP wallets. This relatively low threshold suggests that many XRP holders possess modest amounts, indicating a broad base of retail investors. The concentration of XRP holdings among a small percentage of wallets is a notable characteristic of its market structure, impacting liquidity and price volatility.

data.title: AI: The Game Changer for Small Businesses and Local Economies
data.description:
Artificial intelligence (AI) is transforming the way small businesses operate and grow, ultimately amplifying their impact on local economies. By leveraging AI tools and applications, small businesses can increase productivity, improve decision-making, and foster innovation, leading to enhanced competitiveness and market disruption. This article explores how AI is empowering small businesses and driving economic growth in local communities.

AI adoption by small businesses has surged in recent years, with nearly every small business utilizing a tool enabled by AI (98%), and the use of generative AI tools like chatbots and image creation nearly doubling (40%) from last year (U.S. Chamber of Commerce, 2023). This widespread adoption is driven by the numerous benefits AI offers, including cost savings, improved customer experience, data-driven decision-making, increased productivity, and competitive advantage.

One of the key AI tools and applications that small businesses find most beneficial is customer support chatbots. These AI-powered tools improve customer experiences by automating responses to common inquiries, providing real-time personalization, and improving service availability. By enhancing customer interactions, chatbots help small businesses reduce the workload on human agents and provide 24/7 customer support.

Marketing automation is another crucial AI application for small businesses. AI-driven marketing automation uses data-driven insights, predictive analytics, and personalized recommendations to create targeted marketing campaigns. Small businesses can leverage AI tools like ChatGPT to generate written content for product descriptions, emails, blog posts, and more, saving time and resources while maintaining a consistent online presence.

Inventory management is another area where AI solutions can significantly benefit small businesses. AI solutions for inventory management help small businesses predict demand, efficiently manage items and inventory, and gain a comprehensive view of their stock across multiple areas. This enables better resource allocation and reduces the risk of stockouts or excess inventory, ultimately driving business growth and economic impact.

AI-driven innovations in small businesses also impact competition with larger corporations and market dynamics. By leveraging AI, small businesses can level the playing field, improve decision-making, increase productivity, and gain a competitive advantage. This, in turn, fosters innovation and economic growth in local communities.

In conclusion, AI is a powerful tool for small businesses, enabling them to grow, compete, and drive economic growth in local communities. By embracing AI tools and applications, small businesses can increase productivity, improve decision-making, and foster innovation, ultimately amplifying their impact on local economies. As AI continues to evolve and become more accessible, small businesses should capitalize on this technology to stay competitive and drive long-term success.

data.title: CPS Shares Surge 2.5% on Earnings Boost
Lumen Lights Up: Wells Fargo Upgrades Shares 3.71%!
Chart Plunges 2.22% After Q4 Earnings
Bitcoin and Crypto Stocks Jump 10% on Trump's Reserve Plan
data.description:
Bitcoin surged over 10% from last week's low on Monday, with other cryptocurrencies and crypto-related stocks such as Strategy also posting strong gains. The rally followed a statement from U.S. President Donald Trump, who hinted at the creation of a new U.S. strategic reserve that would include a variety of digital assets.

In a post on Truth Social, Trump revealed that his January executive order on digital assets would facilitate the stockpiling of multiple cryptocurrencies, including Bitcoin, Ethereum, XRP, Solana, and Cardano. Bitcoin and Ethereum would serve as the core assets in this reserve.

Cryptocurrencies faced a sharp selloff last week, as investors dumped major tech stocks like those in the Magnificent 7, dampening market confidence in Bitcoin. Some traders view Bitcoin as a proxy for tech stocks, meaning its price often moves in tandem with the broader technology sector.

How Do Analysts View It?

Trump just gave the pump that crypto traders had been waiting for, said Matt Simpson, senior market analyst at City Index.Any faith that was lost last week appears to have been restored, he added, suggesting that new all-time highs could be reached unless another wave of risk-off sentiment hits the market.

Chris Weston, head of research at Pepperstone, noted that the rally might extend into Trump's first White House Crypto Summit, scheduled for Friday. However, he cautioned that broader market bearishness could still weigh on sentiment.

While Trump's announcement has significantly boosted prices, it has also raised concerns, according to IG market analyst Tony Sycamore.

He pointed out that funding for the U.S. crypto reserve could come from two possible sources: U.S. taxpayers' money, which could spark political and financial debates; Cryptocurrencies seized in law enforcement actions, which would not be as bullish since it represents a mere transfer of assets between accounts rather than new buying entering the market.

data.title: Intel Surges 6% Premarket as Nvidia, Broadcom Test 18A Chips
data.description:
Intel's stock surged 6% in premarket trading, reaching approximately $25 per share and reclaiming a $100 billion market cap, after reports that nvidia and broadcom are conducting chip manufacturing tests with the company.

Two sources familiar with the matter told Reuters that chip designers Nvidia and Broadcom are testing Intel's advanced 18A manufacturing process. If the results are satisfactory, both companies could sign manufacturing contracts worth hundreds of millions of dollars, significantly boosting Intel's revenue.

Intel's 18A Process Competing with TSMC

The 18A process, which has been in development for years, enables the production of advanced AI processors and other high-performance chips. This technology places Intel's capabilities between TSMC's latest process and its previous generation, positioning it as a direct competitor to the Taiwanese semiconductor giant, which dominates the global chip manufacturing market.

Testing remains ongoing and could last several months, though the exact start date is unclear. Unlike full chip design validation, these tests focus on evaluating the performance and capabilities of Intel's 18A process. Chip designers often purchase wafers to test specific components before committing to mass production.

Testing Uncertainty

It's important to note that successful testing is not guaranteed. In 2023, Reuters reported that a batch of Broadcom's initial tests failed to meet expectations, disappointing the company's executives and engineers. However, Broadcom stated at the time that it remained in discussions with Intel's foundry.

Intel's Strategic Edge Amid U.S. Manufacturing Push

While intel has fallen behind in the GPU era, recent developments provide a glimmer of hope. Former U.S. President Donald Trump has emphasized reshoring American manufacturing and competing with China, positioning Intel as the country's key semiconductor player.

Intel has already signed agreements with Microsoft and Amazon to use the 18A process for chip production, though specific details remain undisclosed. Meanwhile, both Broadcom and TSMC have shown interest in splitting Intel's design and manufacturing divisions, but negotiations remain in early stages.

data.title: 5 Technical Bullish Stocks Ready for a Nice Bounce as Sell-off Ends and Cheap Valuations (Bonus Sell List Included)
data.description:
The S&P 500 has posted consecutive weekly losses, driven by concerns over Trump's tariff risks further slow the economy and lackluster Q4 earnings from the Mag 7. Technically, a double top chart pattern has formed, with the index attempting to bottom out, indicating potential volatility ahead. We believe the current correction period is nearing its end, and many stocks are now more reasonably valued following the sell-off, setting the stage for a new technical bull market. To hedge against downside risk, we have also prepared a bonus short stock list at the end.


Nvidia (NVDA)

We are confident that nvidia is a buy now. The stock tumbled 8% last Thursday following what many considered an unsurprising report and declined another 3% on Friday but eventually closed 4% higher. This scenario is reminiscent of August 5th, when the stock tested $100 after several sell-offs and soon rallied 28% in just two weeks.

Regarding Nvidia's latest earnings: the new Blackwell GPU is hot-selling with robust guidance, and China's DeepSeek is driving further demand as more computing power is required for inference. Jensen Huang stated that reasoning AI needs 100 times more AI chips. With amazon, google, and Meta committing to massive AI spending this year, and China tech giants catching up in the AI race, ramping up NVIDIA's H20 chip, these factors support Nvidia's bullish outlook.

Why did the stock face a huge sell-off last week? The stock has been under downward pressure due to macro headwinds, lower risk sentiment from other tech companies' disappointing earnings, and valuation concerns. Nvidia's earnings were the final trigger for some investors to sell shares, even forcing some bulls to cash out. However, Friday's V-turn indicates that bulls are stepping up again, recognizing the strong fundamentals and reasonable valuation given the sell-off. Nvidia's P/E ratio has now dropped to 34, making the valuation more reasonable considering its high growth potential.

From a technical perspective, Nvidia tested its support level last Friday but eventually recovered. The short-term bottom may have already formed, with a potential 13% upside to $140. The stock may face challenges again, but if it can break the downward high-high chart pattern, it is ready for further gains.

Amazon (AMZN)

Amazon remains on our bull list, with the share price returning to December levels after lackluster earnings and currently 13% below the February 4th historical high. However, the stock dropped only 2% last week, outperforming the Nasdaq 100's 3.38% decline, indicating emerging investor interest. Amazon's fundamentals and technicals show strength. The e-commerce and cloud giant would benefit from AI assistants providing more customized recommendations and stimulating online shopping while offering cloud services for tech companies. The CEO also emphasized that cloud capacity is currently affecting growth, supporting further company expansion.

Technically, Amazon's MA(3) downward trend is slowing compared to MA(7,10), implying a potential short-term bounce. If MA(3,7,10) all turn upward, a further rally is likely.

TSMC (TSM)

Our view on TSMC is moderately bullish. The stock is testing the $180 support level, returning to November lows. Apple and Nvidia's strong reliance on TSMC and its dominant position in chip foundry, along with a 50%+ gross margin, make the valuation attractive. However, Trump's tariff risks have lowered TSMC sentiment despite robust business fundamentals. Overall, the demand for AI and consumer electronics will increase in the next few years, supporting TSMC.

Technically, the RSI is around 21, a level not seen since last April. Following that level, the stock experienced a continued rally, marking a bullish uptrend. Investors should closely monitor the MA(3) for a potential turnaround to confirm the onset of a bull market.

Arm Holdings (ARM)

Arm Holdings shares are also attractive after the recent selloff, nearing support levels. Nvidia and Arm both benefit from the AI wave and share similar properties. Its parent company Softbank is collaborating with OpenAI on the Stargate project, further boosting Arm's growth. Additionally, Arm is working on offering custom data center CPUs, with Meta as the first customer, solidifying its fundamentals.

Technically, Arm's RSI fell to 23.8, a level last seen on August 7. After that, the stock jumped 26% in two weeks and maintained an upward trend. As the current macro sell-off nears its end, Arm is poised for a resilient bounce.

Salesforce (CRM)

Salesforce shares have been sliding due to disappointing earnings last week. However, AI monetization efforts resulted in an 18% increase in Q4 net income despite a 9% year-over-year sales growth. Technically, the RSI is around 22, a level last seen on January 10th, after which the shares jumped. Having lost 20% from its historical high, the current valuation and oversold conditions could push the stock higher.

Bonus Short Stock List

To hedge against further sell-offs, here is a list of short stocks with brief summaries (from a fundamental or technical standpoint):

Tesla: Despite technical bounce signals, the fundamentals are weak as Musk's political interventions could deteriorate Tesla's sales and earnings. Robotaxi is still far off, and China's FSD is below expectations, presenting a conflict of interest as Musk also leads DOGE.

Costco: Earnings are due on Thursday, and the stock is currently overvalued. It may face a sell-off similar to Walmart's after earnings, compounded by tariff warnings.

Raytheon: The stock is at a historical high with an RSI of 81. Trump may reduce military spending.

Eli Lilly: Shares are heading to October levels, with a potential Trump investigation looming. The demand for weight loss drugs is also a concern.

data.title: Globe Life Shares Soar: Board Expansion Boosts Confidence
Trump's Tariff Decision: A Ticking Time Bomb for U.S. Markets
data.description:
As the deadline for President Trump's decision on U.S. tariff levels on Mexico and Canada approaches, investors are bracing for potential market volatility. The proposed tariffs, which could reach as high as 25% on goods from both countries, have sparked concerns about their impact on inflation, economic growth, and U.S. industries. Here's a closer look at what's at stake and how the U.S. stock market might react.



Inflation and Economic Growth

The proposed tariffs could have significant implications for inflation and economic growth in the U.S. According to experts, the tariffs could increase prices for consumers and businesses, leading to higher inflation. For instance, a survey from EY-Parthenon found that 50% of business executives said they would pass on two-thirds or more of any increased costs they incur from tariffs to consumers. This could lead to demand destruction and slow economic growth.

Moreover, the uncertainty surrounding these tariffs and potential retaliatory measures could lead to a slowdown in investment and consumer spending, further damaging the U.S. economy. The U.S. Economic Policy Uncertainty Index jumped to 502 on Friday, March 4, 2025, its highest level since March 2020, indicating increased uncertainty. Historically, this level of uncertainty has translated to a 3% reduction in the S&P 500's forward P/E ratio.

Market Reaction

Goldman Sachs analysts estimate that the proposed tariffs could reduce the S&P 500's fair value by about 5%. The U.S. stock market has already reacted negatively to the announcement of these tariffs, with the S&P 500 index coming off its earlier lows but remaining firmly in negative territory on Monday, February 3, 2025.

The most impacted sectors are likely to be Consumer Discretionary, Industrials, Energy, and Technology. Companies in these sectors rely heavily on imported goods and could face higher input costs, reduced profit margins, or lower sales volumes due to tariffs.



Retaliatory Measures and U.S. Industries

Mexico and Canada have vowed to retaliate if the 25% tariffs on their goods are implemented. Potential retaliatory measures include counter tariffs on U.S. goods, which could weaken U.S. industries that rely on exports to these countries. For example, the U.S. auto industry, which is heavily integrated with Mexican and Canadian production, could face significant disruptions. According to a study by the Center for Automotive Research, a 25% tariff on imported vehicles and parts could lead to a loss of 75,000 to 150,000 U.S. jobs in the auto industry.

In conclusion, President Trump's decision on U.S. tariff levels on Mexico and Canada could have significant implications for inflation, economic growth, and the U.S. stock market. Investors should closely monitor the situation and be prepared for potential market volatility. As the deadline approaches, it's crucial to stay informed and adapt investment strategies accordingly.

data.title: Shiba Inu Surges: 40% of Holders 'In the Money'
data.description:
Shiba Inu (SHIB), the popular cryptocurrency, has shown signs of recovery as the market as a whole rebounds. With 40% of its holders now 'in the money,' investor sentiment has significantly improved, marking a notable shift in confidence.

The recent price movement of Shiba Inu highlights growing optimism among its holders, as stated by a representative from COINOTAG. The broader crypto market's rally in March has played a pivotal role in rejuvenating interest in SHIB, with the cryptocurrency experiencing an approximate 4% increase recently.

According to data from IntoTheBlock, the percentage of SHIB holders categorized as 'in the money' has risen to 40%, a dramatic increase from a low of 18%. This suggests enhanced confidence among investors, as more holders in profit typically decreases selling pressure, allowing for potential price appreciation.

Monitoring Shiba Inu's performance reveals that the price is currently around $0.00001416, with a notable rise to $0.0000152 during recent trading sessions. While still down 4.1% on a weekly scale, the momentum showcases potential for an upward trend. Analysts are keeping a close eye on critical resistance levels, particularly the daily moving averages of $0.00001733 and $0.0000192. A breakout above these levels could solidify a bullish outlook for SHIB.

With the recent price action and increasing confidence among holders, analysts suggest that Shiba Inu may be entering a pivotal phase. Additional upside could be enabled as the market stabilizes and traders digest recent developments. Investors should remain vigilant about the shifting market dynamics and watch for any notable changes in volume and holder composition.

As Shiba Inu navigates a recovering market landscape, its recent surge in 'in the money' holders signals growing confidence. Investors watching for key resistance breaks may find new opportunities in SHIB, encouraging a careful yet optimistic view towards future price movements.

data.title: Metacade, Base, and Coinbase Team Up to Revolutionize Onchain Gaming
data.description:
Metacade, a leading onchain gaming ecosystem, has announced a strategic partnership with Base, an Ethereum Layer 2 network developed by coinbase, and Coinbase itself. This collaboration introduces a Layer 3 (L3) Appchain, designed to enhance scalability, reduce costs, and increase efficiency for onchain gaming.

Built on Base’s cutting-edge infrastructure, the Metacade Appchain aims to redefine onchain gaming by leveraging L3 technology to enable faster transactions, lower gas fees, and seamless scalability, accelerating adoption across the industry. Russell Bennett, CEO of Metacade, expressed his excitement about the partnership, stating that it unlocks limitless potential for the platform, empowering game studios, enhancing $MCADE utility, and driving onchain adoption at scale.

The Metacade Appchain offers several advantages for game studios and developers, including faster transactions, lower costs, and scalability. The soon-to-launch Tournament v2 platform will further drive engagement by allowing any Base token to be integrated instantly, without coding. This feature enables tokens as native prize pools in live tournaments, providing a scalable way for communities to boost participation and incentivize growth.

The partnership also strengthens the long-term value of the $MCADE token through token burns, gas token utility, and growth potential. Metacade, the premier onchain gaming ecosystem on Base, connects gamers, builders, and investors through hyper-casual games, tournaments, and creator tools. Powered by $MCADE, Metacade focuses on scalable infrastructure and real-world rewards.

Base, an Ethereum Layer 2 network developed by Coinbase, makes decentralized applications faster, cheaper, and more accessible. Coinbase, a global leader in cryptocurrency, supports millions in advancing the onchain economy. This collaboration between Metacade, Base, and Coinbase is set to transform the onchain gaming landscape, driving innovation and adoption in the industry.
